input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-02T00:55:09.215Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user is dealing with a known behavioral difference between VSTest and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). In MTP, when a test assembly produces zero tests, the test runner returns exit code 8 (which means "no tests found"), causing CI pipelines to fail. With VSTest, this was not an error.

Let me look at their project files to understand the current setup.

data.content:
Exit code **8** in Microsoft.Testing.Platform means **"no tests discovered"** — it treats zero tests as an error, unlike VSTest. Since your tests are conditionally compiled out (via `#if INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS`), some configurations produce an empty assembly.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (enabled via `EnableMSTestRunner`), exit code 8 means "no tests were discovered." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, the entire test class is compiled out, resulting in zero tests, and MTP returns exit code 8.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>` in the project file. This tells MTP to return exit code 0 instead of 8 when no tests are found.


This property was designed specifically for this kind of migration scenario.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.content: The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag works. Now let me set it properly in the project file:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found"; treat it as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**The fix:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest returned 0). Adding this MSBuild property to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

tells the runner to map exit code 8 → 0, so empty assemblies no longer break CI. Real test failures still fail as expected.

If you have multiple test projects, you can put this in a `Directory.Build.props` instead to apply it everywhere.
